evidence: the development and deployment of AI has required and will require additional investment that increases our costs
Danaher s CIO... reports to Danaher s Chief Technology and Artificial Intelligence Officer
These risks are exacerbated by the increasing importance of AI and the increasing incorporation of AI in our business
We are in the early stages of incorporating AI, including machine learning technologies, into our business activities and our product and service offerings
rapid technological development (particularly with respect to computing, automation, AI, mobile connectivity and digitization) in most of the Company s served markets
AI algorithms may be flawed or may be based on datasets that are biased or insufficient or contain errors... disruption or failure in the AI functionality... could adversely impact our business
Incorporating AI into our business operations presents new risks relating to intellectual property, disclosure of confidential data, data protection, data privacy and cybersecurity
Compliance with new or changing laws, regulations or industry standards relating to AI may impose significant costs and may limit our ability to develop, deploy or use AI technologies

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI-specific spend, headcount, or capex figures disclosed in the excerpts; a named Chief Technology and Artificial Intelligence Officer role and acknowledgment of required investment support a score of 3 but not higher.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Company explicitly states it is in 'early stages' of AI incorporation with no named deployed initiatives or use cases described; language is largely aspirational and risk-oriented rather than operational.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section and MD&A material is present and contains detailed, company-specific AI risk mechanisms (IP loss, regulatory penalties, cybersecurity, competitive harm, data privacy) with named regulatory frameworks (GDPR), supporting score 4; no quantified risk exposure or named AI governance/oversight body beyond the CISO/IRSC structure (which is cybersecurity-focused rather than AI-specific).
